_meta.description: Curated dataset of professional Counter-Strike (CS:GO / CS2) team rosters, grouped by Major appearance. Each team-major entry represents a specific roster as they competed at that Major — stats reflect player performance at or around that event. Stats (rating, K/D, ADR, impact) are approximations anchored to real, publicly reported HLTV performance data.
_meta.ratingSystem: Approximates HLTV Rating 2.0/2.1 scale (~1.00 = average top-tier pro, 1.15+ = elite, 1.30+ = legendary peak year)

roles.label: Entry Fragger
roles.description: First in on executes, opens duels

roles.label: Lurker
roles.description: Plays solo angles, flanks, info gathering

roles.label: In-Game Leader
roles.description: Calls strategy, often lower personal stats

teamMajors.blurb: The 87-0 LAN streak team. Untouchable through 2012-13, the first true CS:GO dynasty.
The lineup that ended NiP's 87-0 streak at StarSeries V — Poland's first true CS:GO contender.
The kennyS-led French roster that won the chaotic Cluj-Napoca Major of 2015.
Three Majors in 13 months. The flusha/olof/JW core at their untouchable peak.
The post-87-0 NiP — still dangerous but no longer untouchable, grinding through roster experiments.
The golden generation at their peak — pasha, Snax, byali, NEO and TaZ lifting Poland's first Major trophy.
The dennis-era Fnatic that made the Columbus final — olof and flusha's last great run together before the split.
The Brazilian core that broke Europe's grip on the game, winning back-to-back Majors in 2016.
The f0rest/GeT_RiGhT core in their twilight era — still competitive but no longer contenders.
Three straight Majors. The most statistically dominant team era in CS:GO history.
The only North American Major champions ever — a shocking Boston 2018 run for the ages.
North America's best ever roster. Intel Grand Slam champions who fell just short at Berlin.
Sh1ro's first big stage — a young CIS roster that briefly looked unstoppable before NaVi's peak stopped them.
s1mple's peak. The Stockholm Major champions and arguably the best individual talent ever assembled.
The 2022 Major-winning superteam. Five stars, one trophy that finally validated the project.
s1mple and b1t carrying NaVi through the Antwerp bracket before falling to FaZe in the grand final.
ZywOo's breakout superteam — the new international roster coming together at Rio.
The cadiaN-led core at their most dangerous — always the bridesmaid, pushing deep at Paris.
The m0NESY-led era — a teenage AWP prodigy fronting one of CS2's most consistently elite rosters.
Sh1ro's clinical, fundamentals-first roster that proved CIS talent never left after NaVi's peak faded.
ZywOo plus an international supporting cast — CS2's most dominant roster lifting the Copenhagen trophy.
